Biometric Monitoring

RHR vs Polar · HRV vs WHOOP

_REP's Resting Heart Rate compared against Polar H10, and Heart Rate Variability compared against WHOOP — showing the latest 5 days of continuous simultaneous wear data.

_REP (solid)
Reference device (dashed)

Users 1–3 are live validation data. Users 4+ are preview placeholders showing how the section scales once the backend is connected.

3 Users · 5 Days · Within 2 bpm of Polar (RHR) and WHOOP
  • Average RHR delta across 3 users: <1 bpm vs Polar H10
  • Average HRV delta: ~4.3 ms vs WHOOP HRV — within inter-device measurement norms
  • Both devices captured the same recovery trends, day over day, across all 3 users
  • Latest observation (Jun 24, 2026): RHR matched Polar exactly — Δ 0 bpm across all 3 users

Sleep Tracking

Every Night Tracked. Every Stage Validated.

Validated across 90+ consecutive nights against Apple Watch and WHOOP 4.0 simultaneously. The 5 nights shown below (Jun 19–24) are a representative sample — validation ran continuously for 90+ consecutive nights. All deltas under ±10 minutes.

vs Apple Watch & WHOOP

Hours of Sleep Validated
~10 users · 900+ nights · 3-month study
Sleep Stage Detection — _REP vs Polysomnography (PSG)
PSG (Polysomnography) is the clinical gold standard for sleep staging · Shows how accurately _REP identifies each sleep stage compared to lab-grade measurement
70.5%
Wake Detection
vs PSG benchmark
71.8%
REM Detection
vs PSG benchmark
80.1%
Light Sleep Detection
vs PSG benchmark
42.7%
Deep Sleep Detection
vs PSG benchmark
PSG Says → / _REP Detects ↓WakeREMLightDeep
Wake70.5%3.5%25.8%0.2%
REM5.3%71.8%22.5%0.4%
Light Sleep11.8%4.8%80.1%3.4%
Deep Sleep2.0%1.1%54.2%42.7%

Swipe table sideways for all stages →

How to read: Rows = what PSG (gold standard) says the sleep stage is. Columns = what _REP detected. Highlighted cells on the diagonal = correct detections. _REP excels at Light Sleep (80.1%) and REM (71.8%) — the stages that matter most for recovery insights. Deep sleep is hardest for any wrist-based wearable to detect, consistent with industry benchmarks.

In the lab

Three metrics, still earning it.

We don't ship a number until it survives validation. These are in supervised trials right now — they unlock the moment they pass.
